categorize.confirm_delete_rule: Are you sure you want to delete the rule ":name"?
categorize.confirm_save: Apply this categorization? This will recategorize all URLs.
categorize.help_bp_avoid_broad: Avoid too-broad patterns that could match too many URLs
categorize.help_bp_escape_chars: Escape special characters with \ (e.g. \. for a literal dot)
categorize.help_bp_specific_first: Place specific categories first, generic ones last
categorize.help_bp_test_before: Test your rules before applying with the "Test" button
categorize.help_bp_use_caret: Use ^ to match URL start for precision
categorize.help_intro_text: The cat.yml file lets you automatically categorize your website's URLs based on patterns. Each category contains rules that define which URLs belong to it.
categorize.help_need_help_text: Check that your patterns match the expected URLs using the "Test" button. Uncategorized URLs are those that don't match any rule.
categorize.help_order_warning_text: Categories are evaluated in order. A URL is assigned to the first matching category. Place specific categories before generic ones.
categorize.help_regex_bracket: One of the characters a, b or c
categorize.help_regex_caret: Start of the URL (after the domain)
categorize.help_regex_plus: 1 or more of the preceding character
categorize.help_regex_star: 0 or more of the preceding character
categorize.help_regex_tip: 💡 Tip: Patterns use regular expressions (regex). Here are the most useful symbols:
categorize.help_structure_example:
Category Name:
  dom: example.com
  include:
    - /pattern1
    - /pattern2
  exclude:
    - /exception
categorize.help_workflow_step1: Write your YAML rules in the editor
categorize.help_workflow_step3: Check the preview and adjust your rules
categorize.help_workflow_step4: Click "Save & Apply" to apply the categorization
categorize.msg_saved_batch: Categorization applied (:count URLs). Batch in progress for :crawls other crawl(s)...
categorize.no_rules_hint: Add a rule to start categorizing URLs.

inlinks.chart_distribution_desc: This chart shows the number of inlinks (Y axis) for a given percentage of URLs (X axis). For example, if at 80% there are 5 inlinks, it means 80% of URLs have 5 inlinks or fewer.
inlinks.table_category_desc: Incoming link statistics by page type
inlinks.tooltip: :x% of URLs have :y inlinks or fewer.

login.alert_first_use_desc: Create your administrator account to secure access to Scouter.
login.error_fill_fields: Please fill in all required fields
login.error_password_short: Password must be at least 6 characters
monitor.heading: System Monitor
monitor.subtitle: Real-time job and crawl status
outlinks.chart_distribution_desc: This chart shows the percentage of URLs (Y axis) with at least X outlinks (X axis), starting from pages with the most outlinks. Helps quickly identify pages with too many outgoing links.
outlinks.table_category_desc: Outgoing link statistics by page type
outlinks.tooltip: :x% of URLs have :y outlinks or more.
pagerank.chart_flow_desc: Number of internal dofollow links between categories
pagerank_leak.chart_distribution_desc: PageRank split between indexable, non-indexable and external URLs
pagerank_leak.chart_top_domains_desc: External domains receiving the most PageRank
redirects.disabled_desc: The "Follow redirects" setting was disabled for this crawl.
scope_modal.title: Data Scope
seo_tags.table_issues: Pages with empty or duplicate tags
sql_explorer.display_limited_detail: First :displayed rows out of :total total. Use CSV export or Copy to get all data.
sql_explorer.help_architecture_text: Data is partitioned by crawl. The pages, links and categories tables are virtual tables that automatically point to the current crawl partition.
sql_explorer.help_col_dup_page_count: Number of pages in the cluster
sql_explorer.help_ex_jsonb_text: XPath/Regex extractions are stored as JSONB. Use the ->> operator to extract text values:
sql_explorer.help_ex_structured_data_tex: Schema.org types are stored in the schemas column (array) and the page_schemas table for stats:
sql_explorer.help_feat_autocomplete_desc: Type to see table and column suggestions
sql_explorer.help_feat_export_desc: Export all results (even if display is limited to 500 rows)
sql_explorer.help_feat_saved_desc: Click a predefined query to load it
sql_explorer.help_introduction_text: The SQL Explorer allows you to query the crawl database directly with custom SQL queries. Use smart autocompletion and saved queries to explore your data efficiently.
sql_explorer.help_limit_display_desc: 500 rows maximum for performance (full CSV export available)
sql_explorer.help_limit_timeout_desc: Queries that take too long are interrupted
sql_explorer.help_note_arrays: page_ids and schemas are PostgreSQL arrays (TEXT[]), use ANY() or @>
sql_explorer.help_note_category_join: To get the category name, join with categories
sql_explorer.help_note_compliant: Use compliant = true for indexable pages only
sql_explorer.help_note_crawled: Use crawled = true to filter only crawled pages
sql_explorer.help_note_jsonb: Custom extractions are in JSONB in extracts
sql_explorer.help_note_page_schemas: The page_schemas table allows fast GROUP BY on structured data types
sql_explorer.help_relations_text: Tables are linked by foreign keys:
sql_explorer.help_table_categories_desc: Stores categories and their custom colors:
sql_explorer.help_table_duplicates_desc: Stores groups of duplicate pages (exact or similar):
sql_explorer.help_table_links_desc: Stores all links discovered during crawl:
sql_explorer.help_table_pages_desc: This table contains all information about crawled URLs:
sql_explorer.help_table_schemas_desc: Junction table for Schema.org types (JSON-LD) found on each page:
sql_explorer.help_tip_compliant: Filter on compliant=1 to see only indexable pages
sql_explorer.help_tip_crawled: Filter on crawled=1 to see only crawled pages
sql_explorer.help_tip_jsonb: For JSONB: use ->> to extract text, -> to extract JSON
sql_explorer.help_tip_left_join: Use LEFT JOIN to include URLs without a category
sql_explorer.help_tip_limit: Use LIMIT to limit results during testing
sql_explorer.query_all_links_desc: Complete list of links with source and target
sql_explorer.query_response_codes_desc: Number of URLs per HTTP status code
sql_explorer.query_status_distribution_d: Number of URLs per HTTP status code
sql_explorer.query_top_pagerank_desc: Most popular pages on the site
sql_explorer.run_query_prompt: Execute a SQL query to see results
structured_data.chart_avg_desc: By category, from richest to poorest
structured_data.table_types_desc: Number of pages per Schema.org type
url_table.title: Results
